source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/2023 시험소 규격업데이트/A1417, A1717, F1417/Safety 접수자료 231218/Check list_IEC 60601-1 - 2G Series 231030.docx

document.title: Check list_IEC 60601-1 - 2G Series 231030
document.documentType: Manual and software checklist / IEC 60601-1 test report attachment

sourceSummary.oneLine: Checklist for IEC 60601-1 accompanying documents, PEMS software requirements, and IEC 62304 software lifecycle evidence.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to mark applicable checklist items with verdict P and identify where manual or software documentation explains each requirement.
keyPoints: The manual checklist covers IEC 60601-1 Clause 7.9 requirements for accompanying documents, instructions for use, and technical description.
The software checklist covers IEC 60601-1 Clause 14 requirements for programmable electrical medical systems and PEMS documentation.
The IEC 62304 attachment covers software safety classification, legacy software, development, risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution processes.
A mapping table lists required evidence and manufacturer documents for IEC 62304 clauses.

entities.name: ME EQUIPMENT
entities.evidence: ME EQUIPMENT Identification, marking, and documents

entities.name: PESS
entities.evidence: when application of risk management showed that failure of pess does not lead to unacceptable risk

entities.name: SOUP
entities.evidence: d) software configuration and change management, including soup configuration items and software used to support development

entities.name: Risk management file
entities.evidence: risk management file contains an assessment of risks associated with the failure of the pess

entities.name: Responsible organization
entities.evidence: The responsible organization is referred to this standard for the requirements applicable to me systems

concepts.name: Accompanying documents
concepts.evidence: Me equipment accompanied by documents containing instructions for use, and a technical description

concepts.name: Instructions for use
concepts.pageTarget: Instructions for use for ME equipment
concepts.evidence: Instructions for use include the required information

concepts.name: Technical description
concepts.evidence: Technical description separable from instructions for use contains required information

concepts.name: IT-network connection instructions
concepts.evidence: For pems incorporated into an it-network not validated by the pems manufacturer, instructions made available for implementing the connection

evidence.claim: The checklist asks users to mark applicable Verdict fields with P.
evidence.evidenceText: 해당되는 각 항목의 “Verdict” 란에 “P” 표기해 주시기 바랍니다.

evidence.claim: The manual checklist asks users to identify where each item is explained in the manual.
evidence.evidenceText: 해당되는 각 항목의 “Result – Remark” 란에 manual 어느 부분에 설명되어 있는지 작성 바랍니다.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 Clause 7.9 accompanying documents include instructions for use and a technical description.
evidence.evidenceText: Me equipment accompanied by documents containing instructions for use, and a technical description

evidence.claim: Instructions for use include warnings and safety notices.
evidence.evidenceText: Instructions for use include all warning and safety notices
evidence.pageTarget: Instructions for use for ME equipment

evidence.claim: Instructions for use include cleaning, disinfection, and sterilization methods and applicable tolerated parameters.
evidence.evidenceText: Information provided on cleaning, disinfection, and sterilization methods, and applicable parameters that can be tolerated by me equipment parts or accessories specified

evidence.claim: Instructions for use include preventive inspection, calibration, maintenance, and frequency information.
evidence.evidenceText: Instructions provided on preventive inspection, calibration, maintenance and its frequency

evidence.claim: The technical description includes essential data for safe operation, transport, storage, installation, and preparation for use.
evidence.evidenceText: All essential data provided for safe operation, transport, storage, and measures or conditions necessary for installing me equipment, and preparing it for use

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 Clause 14 applies IEC 62304:2006 and IEC 62304:2006/AMD1:2015 clauses 4.3, 5, 7, 8, and 9 to PESS software development or modification when applicable.
evidence.evidenceText: When the requirements of 14.2 to 14.13 apply, the requirements of IEC 62304:2006 and IEC 62304:2006/amd1:2015 clause 4.3, 5, 7, 8 and 9 apply for the development or modification of software of each pess

evidence.claim: PEMS development lifecycle documentation includes defined milestones.
evidence.evidenceText: A pems development life-cycle including a set of defined milestones has been documented

evidence.claim: The PEMS risk management process includes hazards associated with software, hardware, IT-network incorporation, third-party components, and legacy subsystems.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer considered hazards associated with software and hardware aspects of pems including those associated with the incorporating pems into an it-network, components of third-party origin, legacy subsystems when compiling list of known or foreseeable hazards

evidence.claim: PEMS validation includes validation of basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: A pems validation plan containing validation of basic safety & essential performance

evidence.claim: For PEMS incorporated into an IT-network not validated by the PEMS manufacturer, implementation instructions include purpose, required characteristics, configuration, security specifications, information flow, routing, and hazardous situations from IT-network failure.
evidence.evidenceText: For pems incorporated into an it-network not validated by the pems manufacturer, instructions made available for implementing the connection include the following

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software safety classification assigns each software system a class according to risk of harm in a worst-case hazardous situation.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer assigns to each software system a software safety class according to the risk of harm to the patient, operator, or other people resulting from a hazardous situation to which the software system can contribute in a worst-case-scenario

evidence.claim: The IEC 62304 checklist includes legacy software compliance through clauses 5 through 9 or alternative clauses 4.4.2 through 4.4.5.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 5 through 9 have applied to demonstrate the compliance of legacy software

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software development planning includes processes, deliverables, traceability, configuration and change management, and problem resolution.
evidence.evidenceText: The plan addresses the following: a) the processes to be used in the development of the software system b) the deliverables (includes documentation) of the activities and tasks c) traceability between system requirements, software requirements, software system test, and risk control measures implemented in software d) software configuration and change management, including soup configuration items and software used to support development e) software problem resolution

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software requirements include functional, input/output, interface, alarm, security, user interface, database, installation, IT-network, user maintenance, and regulatory requirements as appropriate.
evidence.evidenceText: As appropriate to the medical device software, the manufacturer includes in the software requirements

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software system testing requires tests covering all software requirements with input stimuli, expected outcomes, pass/fail criteria, and procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer establishes and performs a set of tests, expressed as input stimuli, expected outcomes, pass/fail criteria and procedures, for conducting software system testing, such that all software requirements are covered

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software release requires completion and evaluation of all software verification activities before release.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer ensures that all software verification activities has been completed and the results evaluated before the software is released

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software risk management identifies software items that could contribute to hazardous situations from ISO 14971 medical device risk analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer identifies software items that could contribute to a hazardous situation identified in the medical device risk analysis activity of ISO 14971

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 configuration identification requires a scheme for unique identification of configuration items and versions.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer establishes a scheme for the unique identification of configuration items and their versions to be controlled according to the development and configuration planning specified in 5.1

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 problem reports include criticality and other information that may aid problem resolution.
evidence.evidenceText: Problem reports include a statement of criticality (for example, effect on performance, safety, or security) as well as other information that may aid in the resolution of the problem

evidence.claim: The attachment maps IEC 62304 clauses to required evidence and manufacturer document titles, revision numbers, and dates.
evidence.evidenceText: Software - Mapping of required evidence and manufacturer documents Standard Clause Deliverables Title Revision # Date

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not explicitly stated in the source text; numeric strings in the file path were not treated as official document dates.
Revision or version for the checklist document is not present.
Authority or jurisdiction is ambiguous; source metadata says authoritySignal: general and the text does not name a regulator or jurisdiction.
Several checklist rows contain placeholders such as Software Class, RMF Reference, Title, Revision #, and Date without populated values.
The preprocessed text preserves many checklist verdicts but does not include page numbers, table structure, or completed result-remark references.
